title: Establish database schema and core data models
Build job fetching pipeline
Implement pre-filter and enrichment stages
Create scoring and resume sync subagent system
Implement profile evolution and company discovery
Build web UI foundation and job views
Build web UI management pages
Wire CLI orchestration and project setup
Create SQLite database schema with all tables
description: Create the V2 SQLite database module with all 7 tables (jobs, companies, score_dimensions, feedback, profile_snapshots, career_page_configs, profile_suggestions) and their indices. The module must open with PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL on every connection. This is a new file at pipeline/src/database.py. Schema definitions come directly from the spec CREATE TABLE statements.
acceptance_criteria: Running python -c 'from pipeline.src.database import init_db; init_db("data/test.db")' creates a SQLite file at data/test.db with zero errors.
The created database contains exactly 7 tables: jobs, companies, score_dimensions, feedback, profile_snapshots, career_page_configs, profile_suggestions.
Running PRAGMA journal_mode on the opened connection returns wal.
The jobs table has a UNIQUE constraint on the url column.
The score_dimensions table has a UNIQUE(job_id, pass) constraint.
The feedback table has a CHECK constraint on signal column accepting only thumbs_up and thumbs_down.

All indices defined in the spec exist: idx_jobs_company_id, idx_jobs_source, idx_jobs_posted_at, idx_companies_name, idx_companies_domain, idx_score_dimensions_job_id, idx_score_dimensions_overall, idx_feedback_job_id, idx_career_page_configs_company_id, idx_career_page_configs_status, idx_profile_suggestions_status.
A get_connection() function exists that returns a connection with WAL mode enabled and busy_timeout set to at least 5000ms.
notes: PITFALL: Architecture research notes WAL mode must be enforced on every connection open, not just init.
PITFALL: busy_timeout must be set to handle concurrent writes from parallel subagents.
Both files are NEW.

title: Extend data models with Company and ScoreDimension dataclasses
description: Extend the existing pipeline/src/models.py (ported from V1) to add new fields to the Job dataclass and create new Company, ScoreDimension, Feedback, ProfileSnapshot, CareerPageConfig, and ProfileSuggestion dataclasses. The existing Job class needs source_type, company_id, ats_platform, dedup_hash, last_seen_at, and external_id fields added. All dataclasses must match the DB schema columns exactly.
acceptance_criteria: Job dataclass has all new fields: source_type (str), company_id (Optional[int]), ats_platform (Optional[str]), dedup_hash (Optional[str]), last_seen_at (Optional[str]), external_id (Optional[str]).
Company dataclass exists with fields matching all columns in the companies table.
ScoreDimension dataclass exists with fields matching score_dimensions table. The pass column is named pass_num in Python to avoid reserved word conflict.
Feedback, ProfileSnapshot, CareerPageConfig, and ProfileSuggestion dataclasses exist with fields matching their respective tables.
Running python -c 'from pipeline.src.models import Job, Company, ScoreDimension, Feedback, ProfileSnapshot, CareerPageConfig, ProfileSuggestion' succeeds with no ImportError.
All existing V1 fields on Job remain intact (title, company, url, description, source, location, salary_min, salary_max, posted_at, match_score, match_reasoning, db_id, raw).
notes: pipeline/src/models.py is an EXISTING file (ported from V1) that needs modification.
pipeline/tests/test_models.py is a NEW file.

title: Extend settings module with new API environment variables
description: Extend the existing pipeline/config/settings.py (ported from V1) to add loader functions for new environment variables needed by V2: RapidAPI key (LinkedIn), SerpAPI key (company discovery), Crunchbase API key, and database path configuration. Add python-dotenv loading from a .env file. Create a .env.example template file listing all required and optional env vars.
acceptance_criteria: settings.py loads environment variables using python-dotenv from a .env file at the project root.
A get_rapidapi_key() function returns the RAPIDAPI_KEY env var or raises ValueError with a descriptive message.
A get_serpapi_key() function returns the SERPAPI_KEY env var or raises ValueError with a descriptive message.
A get_crunchbase_key() function returns the CRUNCHBASE_API_KEY env var or raises ValueError with a descriptive message.
A get_db_path() function returns the DB_PATH env var or defaults to data/jobs.db.
A .env.example file exists at the project root listing all env vars: ADZUNA_APP_ID, ADZUNA_APP_KEY, RAPIDAPI_KEY, SERPAPI_KEY, CRUNCHBASE_API_KEY, DB_PATH.
.env is listed in .gitignore.
Existing get_adzuna_credentials(), load_profile(), and load_red_flags() functions still work unchanged.
notes: pipeline/config/settings.py is an EXISTING file that needs modification.
.env.example is a NEW file.
.gitignore is an EXISTING file that needs .env added.
pipeline/tests/test_settings.py is a NEW file.

title: Adapt Adzuna and RemoteOK fetchers for V2 database schema
description: Update the existing ported V1 fetchers (adzuna.py, remoteok.py, base.py) to work with the V2 project structure. Update import paths from config.settings to pipeline.config.settings. Update base.py to include a source_type attribute. Both fetchers must produce raw dicts that the normalizer can convert to V2 Job dataclasses with the new fields (source_type, external_id). No DB writes happen in fetchers - they return raw dicts only.
acceptance_criteria: AdzunaFetcher.fetch() returns a list of dicts with no import errors when called from the pipeline/ directory.
RemoteOKFetcher.fetch() returns a list of dicts with no import errors when called from the pipeline/ directory.
BaseFetcher abstract class has a source_type property that returns one of: api, career_page, ats_feed.
AdzunaFetcher.source_type returns 'api'.
RemoteOKFetcher.source_type returns 'api'.
All imports use the pipeline.config.settings module path (not bare config.settings).
Running pytest pipeline/tests/test_fetchers_api.py passes with mocked HTTP responses.
notes: base.py, adzuna.py, remoteok.py are EXISTING files that need modification.
__init__.py and test file are NEW files.
Fetchers do NOT write to the DB - they return raw dicts for the normalizer.

2026-03-20T16:28:10Z: Include normalizer.py source_type fix (seek-34): all three normalize_* functions need source_type argument added to Job() calls — normalize_mock→source_type='mock', normalize_adzuna→source_type='api', normalize_remoteok→source_type='api'. See seek-34 for details.

title: Add LinkedIn fetcher via RapidAPI integration
description: Create a new LinkedIn fetcher that uses the RapidAPI LinkedIn Jobs API to search for jobs matching profile.yaml title_keywords. The fetcher follows the same BaseFetcher interface as adzuna.py and remoteok.py. It loads the RAPIDAPI_KEY from settings and performs keyword-based job searches. Returns raw dicts for normalization.
acceptance_criteria: LinkedInFetcher class exists in pipeline/src/fetchers/linkedin.py and extends BaseFetcher.
LinkedInFetcher.source_type returns 'api'.
LinkedInFetcher.fetch() reads title_keywords from profile.yaml and makes HTTP requests to the RapidAPI LinkedIn endpoint.
If RAPIDAPI_KEY is not set, LinkedInFetcher.fetch() logs a warning and returns an empty list (does not crash).
Running pytest pipeline/tests/test_fetcher_linkedin.py passes with mocked HTTP responses returning sample LinkedIn job data.
The normalizer module has a new normalize_linkedin() function that converts LinkedIn raw dicts to V2 Job dataclasses.
notes: linkedin.py is a NEW file.
normalizer.py is an EXISTING file that needs a new normalize_linkedin() function added.
test file is NEW.

title: Add ATS feed fetcher for Greenhouse Lever and Ashby
description: Create a new ATS feed fetcher that reads companies from the companies table where ats_platform is set, and fetches structured job data from their ATS JSON feeds. Supports Greenhouse (boards-api.greenhouse.io), Lever (api.lever.co), and Ashby (api.ashbyhq.com). Each ATS returns structured JSON that does not need scraping. The fetcher queries the DB for target companies and their ATS platform type.
acceptance_criteria: ATSFetcher class exists in pipeline/src/fetchers/ats.py and extends BaseFetcher.
ATSFetcher.source_type returns 'ats_feed'.
ATSFetcher accepts a database connection in its constructor to query companies with non-null ats_platform.
For a company with ats_platform='greenhouse', the fetcher hits boards-api.greenhouse.io/v1/boards/{company_slug}/jobs and returns parsed job dicts.
For a company with ats_platform='lever', the fetcher hits api.lever.co/v0/postings/{company_slug} and returns parsed job dicts.
For a company with ats_platform='ashby', the fetcher hits api.ashbyhq.com/posting-api/job-board/{company_slug} and returns parsed job dicts.
If any single company ATS request fails, the error is logged and the fetcher continues with remaining companies.
Running pytest pipeline/tests/test_fetcher_ats.py passes with mocked HTTP responses.
notes: ats.py is a NEW file.
normalizer.py is an EXISTING file needing normalize_ats() additions for each platform.
test file is NEW.
This crumb depends on database.py and models.py being available for querying companies.

title: Add career page crawler with scrape strategy execution
description: Create a career page crawler that iterates career_page_configs where status='active', uses the stored scrape_strategy JSON to extract job listings from career page HTML. If extraction returns 0 results for a previously-working config, the crawler flags it as potentially broken by updating the status. The scrape_strategy JSON contains CSS selectors and URL patterns generated by the company discovery subagent.
acceptance_criteria: CareerPageFetcher class exists in pipeline/src/fetchers/career_page.py and extends BaseFetcher.
CareerPageFetcher.source_type returns 'career_page'.
CareerPageFetcher accepts a database connection to query career_page_configs with status='active'.
For each active config, the fetcher requests the URL and applies the scrape_strategy CSS selectors to extract job listings.
If extraction returns 0 results for a config that previously had results (last_crawled_at is not NULL), the config status is updated to 'broken' in the database.
last_crawled_at is updated to current timestamp after each successful crawl attempt.
Running pytest pipeline/tests/test_fetcher_career_page.py passes with mocked HTML responses and sample scrape_strategy JSON.
notes: career_page.py is a NEW file.
normalizer.py needs normalize_career_page() added.
test file is NEW.
PITFALL: Career page DOM changes cause silent 0-result returns. Detection logic must check last_crawled_at to distinguish first-run from regression.

title: Adapt deduplicator for database-backed URL and fuzzy dedup
description: Rewrite the existing V1 deduplicator.py to switch from JSON-file-based seen_jobs tracking to database-backed deduplication. Primary dedup uses the URL UNIQUE constraint on the jobs table. Secondary fuzzy dedup uses dedup_hash (normalized lowercase title + company SHA256). The deduplicator inserts new jobs into the DB and updates last_seen_at for existing jobs. It also computes and stores the dedup_hash for cross-source matching.
acceptance_criteria: A deduplicate_and_insert(jobs, db_connection) function accepts a list of Job dataclasses and a DB connection.
Jobs with a URL already in the jobs table are not inserted; instead their last_seen_at is updated to current timestamp.
Jobs with a new URL are inserted into the jobs table with all fields populated.
dedup_hash is computed as SHA256 of lowercase(title) + lowercase(company) with whitespace normalized.
If a new job has the same dedup_hash as an existing job (different URL, same title+company), the function logs a warning about potential cross-source duplicate but still inserts it.
The old JSON-file-based functions (load_seen_hashes, save_seen_hashes, filter_new_only) are removed.
Running pytest pipeline/tests/test_deduplicator.py passes using an in-memory SQLite database.
notes: deduplicator.py is an EXISTING file that needs a full rewrite.
test file is NEW.
PITFALL: Architecture research flags that dedup_hash normalization must be consistent across all fetchers. Use lowercase + strip + collapse whitespace.

title: Adapt V1 filter module as deterministic pre-filter stage
description: Update the existing ported V1 filter.py to work as the Stage 1.5 deterministic pre-filter. Update import paths to use pipeline.config.settings. The filter reads unfiltered jobs from the database (rather than receiving a list), applies red flag keyword matching, salary minimum check, intern/junior role exclusion, and job age check. Jobs that fail pre-filtering are marked in the database so the scoring stages skip them. Add a pre_filter_status column or use a convention (e.g. overall=-1 in score_dimensions pass=0) to mark filtered-out jobs.
acceptance_criteria: A run_prefilter(db_connection) function exists that reads unscored jobs from the jobs table.
Jobs matching red_flags.yaml keywords or phrases are marked as pre-filtered in the database.
Jobs with salary below profile.yaml salary_min are marked as pre-filtered.
Jobs with intern/internship/co-op/coop in the title are marked as pre-filtered.
Jobs older than max_job_age_days from profile.yaml are marked as pre-filtered.
All imports use pipeline.config.settings module path.
The existing filter functions (should_filter, meets_salary_requirement, is_intern_role, is_too_old) are preserved and reused.
Running pytest pipeline/tests/test_prefilter.py passes using an in-memory SQLite database with sample job rows.
notes: filter.py is an EXISTING file that needs modification.
test file is NEW.
The sys.path manipulation in the current filter.py should be removed in favor of proper package imports.

title: Create enrichment source modules for company metadata
description: Create four enrichment source modules that each independently fetch company metadata and write to the companies table. Each module has a standard interface: enrich(company_id, company_name, db_connection) -> bool. Sources: Crunchbase (size, funding, industry), Glassdoor (rating, culture), levels.fyi (comp data), StackShare + job desc analysis (tech stack). Each source handles its own errors and returns False on failure.
acceptance_criteria: pipeline/src/enrichment/crunchbase.py exists with an enrich() function that queries Crunchbase free tier API for company size, funding stage, industry, and writes results to the companies table.
pipeline/src/enrichment/glassdoor.py exists with an enrich() function that fetches Glassdoor rating and URL for a company and writes to companies.glassdoor_rating and companies.glassdoor_url.
pipeline/src/enrichment/levelsfy.py exists with an enrich() function that fetches compensation data and writes to companies.crunchbase_data as a JSON blob containing comp ranges.
pipeline/src/enrichment/stackshare.py exists with an enrich() function that fetches tech stack data and writes to companies.tech_stack as a JSON array.
Each enrich() function returns True on success and False on failure (API down, no data, rate limited).
Each module logs a warning message when its enrichment source is unavailable rather than raising an exception.
Running pytest pipeline/tests/test_enrichment_sources.py passes with mocked HTTP responses for each source.
notes: All files are NEW.
PITFALL: All enrichment sources are best-effort per spec. Each must handle API failures gracefully.
PITFALL: Crunchbase free tier, Glassdoor, and levels.fyi have access limitations. Implementation should treat all as best-effort.

title: Create enrichment orchestrator with failure handling
description: Create the enrichment orchestrator that runs all four enrichment sources for companies that have no enrichment or stale enrichment (enriched_at older than 30 days). The orchestrator iterates companies from the database, calls each enrichment source independently, and updates the enriched_at timestamp on completion. Partial enrichment is accepted - if one source fails, others still proceed.
acceptance_criteria: pipeline/src/enrichment/orchestrator.py exists with a run_enrichment(db_connection) function.
The orchestrator queries companies where enriched_at IS NULL or enriched_at is older than 30 days.
For each qualifying company, it calls all four enrichment functions (crunchbase, glassdoor, levelsfy, stackshare) independently.
If one enrichment source fails, the orchestrator logs the failure and continues with the remaining sources for that company.
After all sources have been attempted for a company, enriched_at is updated to current timestamp regardless of partial failures.
The orchestrator returns a summary dict with keys: companies_processed (int), sources_succeeded (dict of source_name -> count), sources_failed (dict of source_name -> count).
Running pytest pipeline/tests/test_enrichment_orchestrator.py passes with mocked enrichment sources.
notes: All files are NEW.
PITFALL: enrichment/orchestrator.py must define per-source rate limit constants and add exponential backoff per pitfall research.
Architecture research: partial enrichment is a deliberate design choice, not a gap.

title: Implement resume sync with PDF extraction and prompt template
description: Implement the resume sync stage: Python pre-processing uses pdfplumber to extract raw text from the resume PDF, writes it to a temp file. Create the resume_parser.md subagent prompt template that instructs the subagent to extract skills, experience timeline, accomplishments, and seniority indicators from the text, structure as JSON matching the extracted_skills schema, diff against profile.yaml, and write results to profile_snapshots table and suggestions to profile_suggestions. The Python module detects when the resume PDF hash differs from the latest profile_snapshots entry.
acceptance_criteria: pipeline/src/resume_sync.py exists with a check_resume_changed(db_connection, resume_path) function that returns True when the SHA256 hash of the PDF file differs from the latest profile_snapshots.resume_hash.
pipeline/src/resume_sync.py has an extract_resume_text(resume_path) function that uses pdfplumber to extract text and returns a string.
The extract_resume_text function writes extracted text to a tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile and returns the temp file path.

pipeline/prompts/resume_parser.md exists with a structured prompt that instructs a subagent to parse extracted resume text into JSON with keys: skills (list), experience_timeline (list of dicts), accomplishments (list), seniority_indicators (list).
The prompt includes instructions to write results to profile_snapshots and profile_suggestions tables.
On first run when profile_snapshots is empty, check_resume_changed returns True.
Running pytest pipeline/tests/test_resume_sync.py passes with a sample PDF and mocked pdfplumber output.
notes: All files are NEW.
PITFALL: profile_hash comparison returns NULL on first run when profile_snapshots is empty. Must handle bootstrap case.
PITFALL: temp file from pdfplumber must use context manager to avoid leaks per pitfall research.
pdfplumber must be installed via requirements.txt.

title: Create fast filter Pass 1 scoring prompt and orchestration
description: Create the Pass 1 fast filter scoring system. This includes: the fast_filter.md subagent prompt template that receives a batch of ~40 jobs and returns yes/no + confidence for each, and the Python orchestration code that queries unscored jobs (and stale-scored jobs) from the DB, formats them for the subagent, and writes Pass 1 results to score_dimensions. Jobs below threshold get pass=1 overall=0. Jobs above threshold get pass=1 with their confidence score, making them eligible for Pass 2. When the unscored pool exceeds 40, jobs are split into batches of 40 and 2-3 subagents run in parallel.
acceptance_criteria: pipeline/prompts/fast_filter.md exists with a structured prompt that receives a batch of job descriptions and profile data, and returns a JSON array of objects with job_id, decision (yes/no), confidence (0-100), and brief reasoning.
pipeline/src/scorer.py exists with a get_unscored_jobs(db_connection) function that returns jobs with no score_dimensions row.

pipeline/src/scorer.py has a get_stale_scored_jobs(db_connection) function that returns jobs whose score_dimensions.profile_hash differs from the current profile_hash (computed from profile.yaml + latest profile_snapshot). These jobs are re-queued for Pass 1.
pipeline/src/scorer.py has a write_pass1_results(db_connection, results) function that inserts rows into score_dimensions with pass=1.
Jobs marked 'no' in Pass 1 get overall=0 in score_dimensions.
Jobs marked 'yes' in Pass 1 get overall set to the confidence score.
The UNIQUE(job_id, pass) constraint is respected; re-running Pass 1 for the same job upserts the existing row.
When the combined unscored + stale pool exceeds 40 jobs, split_into_batches() divides them into batches of 40. Up to 3 batches can be sent to parallel subagents.
Running pytest pipeline/tests/test_scorer.py passes with mocked DB data (no actual LLM calls).
notes: All files are NEW.
GAP RESOLUTION (stale re-scoring): get_stale_scored_jobs() detects profile_hash mismatch and includes those jobs in the Pass 1 pool alongside truly unscored jobs.
GAP RESOLUTION (overflow batching): When pool > 40, split into 40-job batches and run 2-3 subagents in parallel for faster throughput.
PITFALL: Write a sentinel row before submission to mark in-flight jobs, clean up on retry.

title: Create deep scoring Pass 2 prompt and parallel orchestration
description: Create the Pass 2 deep analysis scoring system. This includes: the deep_scorer.md subagent prompt template that receives job details + company enrichment data + full profile snapshot, and returns all 5 dimension scores (0-100) with per-dimension reasoning. The Python orchestration code queries Pass 1 survivors, splits them across 3-4 batches for parallel subagents, and writes Pass 2 results to score_dimensions. Profile_hash is computed and stored for staleness detection.
acceptance_criteria: pipeline/prompts/deep_scorer.md exists with a structured prompt that receives job details, company enrichment, and profile data, and returns JSON with role_fit, skills_gap, culture_signals, growth_potential, comp_alignment (each 0-100), overall (weighted composite), and reasoning (per-dimension explanations).
The prompt specifies score weighting: role_fit 30%, skills_gap 25%, culture_signals 15%, growth_potential 15%, comp_alignment 15%.

The prompt includes fallback instructions: if no Glassdoor data, score culture_signals from job description signals only; if no comp data, score comp_alignment from posted salary vs profile expectations only.
pipeline/src/scorer.py has a get_pass1_survivors(db_connection) function that returns jobs where score_dimensions.pass=1 AND overall > 0.
pipeline/src/scorer.py has a split_into_batches(jobs, n_batches) function that divides jobs into roughly equal batches.
pipeline/src/scorer.py has a write_pass2_results(db_connection, results) function that inserts rows into score_dimensions with pass=2 and all 5 dimension scores.
profile_hash is computed as SHA256 of profile.yaml contents + latest profile_snapshot.extracted_skills JSON and stored in each score_dimensions row.
Running pytest pipeline/tests/test_scorer_deep.py passes with mocked DB data.
notes: scorer.py extends the file created in the fast filter crumb.
deep_scorer.md and test file are NEW.

GAP RESOLUTION (stale re-scoring): Pass 2 also respects staleness. get_pass1_survivors() includes jobs that passed Pass 1 but have a stale Pass 2 profile_hash. Stale Pass 2 rows are upserted on re-score.
PITFALL: Parallel subagents writing to score_dimensions may contend. Each subagent should write in a single transaction at end of batch.
PITFALL: profile_hash must handle empty profile_snapshots table on first run.

title: Implement profile evolution analysis and suggestion generation
description: Create the profile evolution stage that runs when there are 20+ new feedback signals since the last evolution check. Also includes an apply_approved_suggestions step that runs at the start of each pipeline run: reads approved suggestions from profile_suggestions, applies their suggested_change JSON diffs to profile.yaml, and marks them as applied. Create the profile_evolution.md subagent prompt template that analyzes feedback patterns (thumbs_up/thumbs_down with associated job scores) against the current profile.yaml and generates specific profile_suggestions. The Python module tracks the last evolution check timestamp and determines eligibility. Suggestions are written to the profile_suggestions table with type, description, reasoning, and suggested_change JSON.
acceptance_criteria: pipeline/src/profile_evolution.py exists with a should_run_evolution(db_connection) function that returns True when there are 20+ feedback rows created after the last profile_snapshots.created_at timestamp.
When profile_snapshots table is empty, should_run_evolution counts all feedback rows.
pipeline/src/profile_evolution.py has a get_feedback_with_scores(db_connection) function that joins feedback with score_dimensions and jobs to provide full context for the subagent.

pipeline/src/profile_evolution.py has an apply_approved_suggestions(db_connection, profile_yaml_path) function that reads all profile_suggestions where status='approved', applies each suggested_change JSON diff to the YAML file, updates status to 'applied', and sets resolved_at timestamp.
apply_approved_suggestions writes the updated YAML back to disk atomically (write to temp file then rename).
If applying a suggestion fails (e.g., target key no longer exists in YAML), the suggestion status is set to 'conflict' with a note, and processing continues with remaining suggestions.

pipeline/prompts/profile_evolution.md exists with a structured prompt that receives feedback+score data and current profile.yaml, and returns a JSON array of suggestion objects with suggestion_type, description, reasoning, and suggested_change fields.
suggestion_type values follow the spec: add_skill, remove_skill, adjust_weight, add_keyword, etc.
suggested_change is a JSON object describing the specific YAML diff to apply.
Running pytest pipeline/tests/test_profile_evolution.py passes with mocked DB data.
notes: All files are NEW.
GAP RESOLUTION (apply mechanism): apply_approved_suggestions() runs in the pipeline before scoring. Python owns all profile.yaml writes, keeping file modifications in one process.
Architecture research: the 20-signal threshold cursor is tracked by comparing feedback.created_at against latest profile_snapshots.created_at. No separate cursor table needed.
PITFALL: feedback table allows duplicate signals per job. Evolution subagent prompt should note this.
PITFALL: YAML round-trip must preserve comments and formatting. Use ruamel.yaml if available, else pyyaml with care.

title: Implement company discovery with ATS detection and scrape strategy
description: Create the company discovery flow: (1) Python search using SerpAPI to find company careers page, (2) company_discovery.md subagent prompt for ATS detection and scrape strategy generation, (3) validation step. When a company name or URL is provided, the system searches for the career page, detects if it maps to a known ATS platform (Greenhouse, Lever, Workday, Ashby), and either creates an ATS feed config or generates a scrape_strategy JSON. Includes maintenance logic: if a previously-working career page config returns 0 results, re-run discovery once then mark broken.
acceptance_criteria: pipeline/src/company_discovery.py exists with a discover_company(company_name, db_connection, career_url=None) function.
If career_url is None, the function uses SerpAPI to search for '{company_name} careers jobs' and extracts the career page URL.
If SerpAPI key is not set, the function logs a warning and returns None (does not crash).

pipeline/prompts/company_discovery.md exists with a structured prompt that receives career page HTML and returns JSON with: is_ats (bool), ats_platform (string or null), ats_slug (string or null), scrape_strategy (JSON object with css_selectors, url_patterns, field_extraction_rules) if not ATS.
For ATS-detected pages, the function creates a row in career_page_configs with the ATS feed URL and updates companies.ats_platform.
For non-ATS pages, the function stores the scrape_strategy JSON in career_page_configs.
A rediscover_broken(db_connection) function re-runs discovery for career_page_configs where status='broken', updating status to 'active' on success or leaving as 'broken' on second failure.
Running pytest pipeline/tests/test_company_discovery.py passes with mocked SerpAPI and HTML responses.
notes: All files are NEW.
PITFALL: Career page scrape_strategy is LLM-generated CSS selectors. Target site DOM changes cause silent 0-result returns.
Architecture research: re-run discovery is a subagent task triggered from within the pipeline run, creating a mixed-execution boundary.

title: Scaffold Next.js app with SQLite connection and shared layout
description: Initialize the Next.js application in the web/ directory using create-next-app with App Router, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. Set up better-sqlite3 for synchronous SQLite reads in server components. Create the shared layout with navigation. Install shadcn/ui and base components (Button, Card, Badge, Table). Create the TypeScript types file mirroring the DB schema. The SQLite connection must open with WAL mode.
acceptance_criteria: Running 'npm run dev' from the web/ directory starts a development server on localhost:3000 without errors.
web/lib/db.ts exports a getDb() function that returns a better-sqlite3 Database instance connected to data/jobs.db with WAL mode enabled.
web/lib/types.ts exports TypeScript interfaces: Job, Company, ScoreDimension, Feedback, ProfileSnapshot, CareerPageConfig, ProfileSuggestion matching the DB schema columns.
web/app/layout.tsx renders a navigation bar with links to: Dashboard (/), Jobs (/jobs), Companies (/companies), Profile (/profile), Feedback (/feedback).
Navigating to localhost:3000 displays the layout with navigation bar and a placeholder Dashboard page.
shadcn/ui Button, Card, Badge, and Table components are installed and importable from web/components/ui/.
web/package.json includes dependencies: next, react, react-dom, better-sqlite3, recharts, tailwindcss.
notes: All files are NEW (created via create-next-app then customized).
Stack research: Next.js latest is 16.2.0 which is fine per spec floor of 14+.
Stack research: better-sqlite3 is synchronous and compatible with server components but NOT edge runtime.
PITFALL: db.ts must set WAL mode on connection open, matching database.py behavior.

title: Build dashboard page with summary stats and alerts
description: Build the Dashboard page at web/app/page.tsx as a server component that reads summary statistics from SQLite. Display: total jobs count, scored-this-run count, average scores, new high-match count. Show alerts for: broken career page configs, pending profile suggestions, resume out of sync. Show quick access to latest high-scoring jobs (top 5 by overall score).
acceptance_criteria: Navigating to localhost:3000 displays a dashboard with summary stat cards showing: total jobs (COUNT from jobs table), jobs scored (COUNT from score_dimensions WHERE pass=2), average overall score (AVG from score_dimensions WHERE pass=2).
An alerts section displays a warning badge when career_page_configs has rows with status='broken'.
An alerts section displays a badge when profile_suggestions has rows with status='pending'.
A 'Top Matches' section displays up to 5 jobs ordered by score_dimensions.overall DESC (pass=2), showing job title, company, and overall score.
Each job in the Top Matches section links to /jobs/{id}.
If the database has zero jobs, the dashboard displays a message indicating no jobs have been fetched yet instead of showing empty stats.

Filters: overall score range, individual dimension ranges, company, source, date range, feedback status. Click a row to navigate to the job detail page. Use shadcn/ui Table component and the dimension-bars component for inline score visualization.

The table is sortable by clicking column headers for: overall score, salary, posted date.

Filter controls exist for: overall score range (slider or min/max inputs), company name (text input), source (dropdown: adzuna, remoteok, linkedin, career_page, ats_feed), feedback status (dropdown: all, thumbs_up, thumbs_down, no_feedback).
Applying a filter refreshes the table to show only matching jobs.
Each row in the table shows mini horizontal bars for the 5 score dimensions using the dimension-bars component.
Clicking a job row navigates to /jobs/{id}.
If no jobs match the current filters, the table displays 'No jobs match your filters' instead of an empty table.

description: Build the Job Detail page at web/app/jobs/[id]/page.tsx. Display: overall score prominently, five dimension scores as radar chart or horizontal bars (using Recharts), per-dimension reasoning expandable sections, company enrichment sidebar (size, funding, rating, tech stack), comp comparison chart (posted range vs market data), thumbs up/down feedback buttons with optional note, and link to original posting. Feedback submission uses a server action to write to the feedback table.

The overall score is displayed prominently at the top of the page.
Five dimension scores (role_fit, skills_gap, culture_signals, growth_potential, comp_alignment) are displayed as a Recharts radar chart or horizontal bar chart.
Each dimension has an expandable section showing the reasoning text from score_dimensions.reasoning JSON.
A company sidebar displays: company size_range, funding_stage, glassdoor_rating, and tech_stack (parsed from JSON array).
Thumbs up and thumbs down buttons exist. Clicking either button with an optional note text field submits a server action that inserts a row into the feedback table.
After submitting feedback, the page shows the current feedback state (thumbs up or down icon).
A 'View Original' link opens the job's URL in a new tab.

Provide an 'Add Company' form that accepts company name or URL, and creates a row in the companies table via server action. Show per-company career page config status. Allow editing/overriding career page URLs. Link to view all jobs from a specific company.

Approve/reject actions use server actions to update profile_suggestions status.

A 'Pending Suggestions' section lists profile_suggestions rows where status='pending', each showing: suggestion_type, description, reasoning, and the suggested_change.

Show job title, company, scores, and any notes. Include summary patterns section if available from the last evolution analysis.

title: Create CLI entry point with stage subcommands
description: Create the pipeline CLI entry point at pipeline/cli.py with argparse subcommands: --fetch (run all fetchers and dedup), --enrich (run enrichment orchestrator), --prefilter (run deterministic pre-filter), --all (fetch + enrich + prefilter in sequence). Each subcommand initializes the database, runs its stage, and prints a summary. The CLI is the entry point for Python data plumbing stages only; LLM stages are handled by Claude Code subagents separately.

Running 'python pipeline/cli.py --enrich' executes the enrichment orchestrator on companies needing enrichment.
Running 'python pipeline/cli.py --prefilter' runs the deterministic pre-filter on unfiltered jobs.
Running 'python pipeline/cli.py --all' runs fetch, enrich, and prefilter in sequence.
Each subcommand prints a summary at completion (e.g., 'Fetched 150 jobs, 45 new. Enriched 12 companies. Pre-filtered 30 jobs.').

Running 'python pipeline/cli.py' with no arguments prints help text.

The CLI help text should clarify that scoring and other LLM stages run via Claude Code subagents, not the CLI.

title: Create Makefile and requirements files for project setup

Create pipeline/requirements.txt with all Python dependencies. Ensure the project can be bootstrapped from scratch with 'make setup' followed by 'make web'.

A README.md exists with setup instructions listing prerequisites (Python 3.10+, Node.js 20+), setup steps, and usage examples.

This is a 3-file crumb which is below the typical minimum. Justified because Makefile, requirements.txt, and README are configuration/documentation files with straightforward content but important integration value.
Stack research: Python 3.14.2 is installed; Node.js 25.5.0 is installed.

title: Fix DB_PATH computation in web/lib/db.ts to anchor to __dirname and respect DB_PATH env var
description:
## Root Cause

`web/lib/db.ts:14` computes DB_PATH using `path.resolve(process.cwd(), "..", "data", "jobs.db")`. This anchors the path to the Node.js process's working directory at import time, not to the file's own location. When `npm run dev` is started from the repo root or from CI (rather than `cd web && npm run dev`), `process.cwd()` resolves to the wrong directory and `better-sqlite3` silently creates a new empty database one level above the repo root instead of connecting to `data/jobs.db`. Additionally, `pipeline/config/settings.py:133` reads DB_PATH from the environment (with `data/jobs.db` as fallback), but `web/lib/db.ts` ignores `process.env.DB_PATH` entirely — any developer-set DB_PATH override has no effect on the web layer. `next.config.ts:8` already uses `path.resolve(__dirname)` for a similar reason.

## Affected Surfaces

- `web/lib/db.ts:14` — `path.resolve(process.cwd(), "..", "data", "jobs.db")` (from edge-cases + drift reviews)
- `pipeline/config/settings.py:133` — reference implementation using DB_PATH env var (from drift review)

## Fix

Replace the hardcoded path constant with:
```typescript
const DB_PATH = process.env.DB_PATH ?? path.resolve(__dirname, "../../data/jobs.db");
```
This navigates from `web/lib/` up two levels to the project root regardless of the CWD at startup, and respects any DB_PATH env var set in the environment.

## Changes Needed

- `web/lib/db.ts:14`: Replace `path.resolve(process.cwd(), "..", "data", "jobs.db")` with `process.env.DB_PATH ?? path.resolve(__dirname, "../../data/jobs.db")`
- `web/next.config.ts`: Verify DB_PATH is accessible as a server-side env var (no NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix needed for server-only use)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Starting `npm run dev` from the repo root connects to `data/jobs.db`, not `../data/jobs.db`
- [ ] Setting DB_PATH to a custom path in the environment causes `web/lib/db.ts` to use that path
- [ ] `next.config.ts` still resolves its own paths correctly (no regression)

title: Add busy_timeout = 5000 pragma to web/lib/db.ts connection to prevent SQLITE_BUSY errors
description:
## Root Cause

`web/lib/db.ts:34` sets `journal_mode = WAL` but never sets `busy_timeout`. SQLite's default busy_timeout is 0 ms — any lock contention immediately returns SQLITE_BUSY rather than retrying. `pipeline/src/database.py:221` sets `PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000` on every connection, and database.py's module docstring (lines 6–7) explicitly states this is required for "concurrent readers from the Next.js UI." During WAL checkpoints (which briefly take an exclusive lock), web-side queries will immediately fail with SQLITE_BUSY rather than waiting up to 5 seconds for the pipeline write to finish.

## Affected Surfaces

- `web/lib/db.ts:34` — WAL pragma set, busy_timeout absent (from edge-cases + drift reviews)
- `pipeline/src/database.py:221` — Python side correctly sets busy_timeout = 5000 on every connection

## Fix

Add `db.pragma("busy_timeout = 5000")` immediately after the WAL pragma in `getDb()`.

## Changes Needed

- `web/lib/db.ts:34-35`: Add `db.pragma("busy_timeout = 5000")` after `db.pragma("journal_mode = WAL")`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `web/lib/db.ts` calls `pragma("busy_timeout = 5000")` on every new connection
- [ ] Under concurrent pipeline writes, the web connection waits up to 5 seconds before failing (matches Python behavior)
- [ ] Existing WAL pragma behavior unchanged

title: Rename Job.raw to Job.raw_json to match DB column name and TypeScript type
description:
## Root Cause

The Python `Job` dataclass uses the field name `raw` (a V1 holdover, typed `Optional[dict]`) at `pipeline/src/models.py:73`, while the SQLite `jobs` table column is `raw_json` (TEXT, storing JSON-serialized data — `pipeline/src/database.py:72`). The TypeScript interface already uses `raw_json: string | null` (`web/lib/types.ts:27`). Any code that iterates `dataclasses.asdict(job)` and passes the result directly to an INSERT statement — as the not-yet-written deduplicator (seek-8) and fetcher adapters (seek-4, seek-5) will do — will produce a "column not found" error or silently write to the wrong column, with no dict→JSON serialization step.

## Affected Surfaces

- `pipeline/src/models.py:73` — `raw: Optional[dict]` (stale V1 name; from drift review)
- `pipeline/src/database.py:72` — `raw_json TEXT` (authoritative DB column name)
- `web/lib/types.ts:27` — `raw_json: string | null` (already correct)

## Fix

Rename the Python field to `raw_json: Optional[str]` (storing serialized JSON text, matching the DB column type). Add a `@property raw` that returns `json.loads(self.raw_json)` for any callers that need a dict. Update test fixtures that construct `Job(raw=...)`.

## Changes Needed

- `pipeline/src/models.py:73`: Rename `raw: Optional[dict]` → `raw_json: Optional[str]`
- `pipeline/src/models.py`: Add `@property raw` returning `json.loads(self.raw_json) if self.raw_json else None`
- Any test fixture using `Job(raw=...)`: update to `raw_json=json.dumps(...)`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `Job.raw_json` field exists and is typed `Optional[str]`
- [ ] `dataclasses.asdict(job)` produces a dict with key `raw_json`, not `raw`
- [ ] A `raw` property exists for backward compatibility and returns a dict or None
- [ ] All existing tests pass after the rename

title: Fix Job.description to Optional[str] to match nullable DB column and TypeScript type
description:
## Root Cause

The three layers of the stack disagree on whether `Job.description` can be NULL. The SQLite schema at `pipeline/src/database.py:65` defines `description TEXT` with no NOT NULL constraint. The TypeScript interface at `web/lib/types.ts:20` correctly mirrors this as `description: string | null`. But the Python dataclass at `pipeline/src/models.py:42` declares `description: str` — a required, non-nullable field with no default. A job row with NULL description — valid at the DB and TypeScript levels — cannot be used to construct a Python `Job` object (Python receives `None` where `str` is expected, causing a type mismatch at runtime). When seek-4 and seek-5 fetch jobs from external APIs, some listings will have no description field.

## Affected Surfaces

- `pipeline/src/models.py:42` — `description: str` (required, non-null; from drift review)
- `pipeline/src/database.py:65` — `description TEXT` (no NOT NULL constraint; referenced by drift)
- `web/lib/types.ts:20` — `description: string | null` (already correct; referenced by drift)

## Fix

Change `pipeline/src/models.py:42` from `description: str` to `description: Optional[str] = None`. Update test fixtures that pass `description` as a required positional argument.

## Changes Needed

- `pipeline/src/models.py:42`: `description: str` → `description: Optional[str] = None`
- `pipeline/tests/test_models.py`: Update any Job fixture that passes description positionally to use a keyword argument (no functional change needed, just signature update)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `Job(title="x", company="y", url="z", source="s", location="l")` constructs successfully without description
- [ ] `Job.description` can be `None`, matching the DB schema and TypeScript type
- [ ] All existing tests pass after the change
- [ ] DB schema and TypeScript type require no changes (already nullable)

title: Unify test import strategy with pyproject.toml pythonpath to fix cross-directory ModuleNotFoundError
description:
## Root Cause

Three test files in the same directory (`pipeline/tests/`) use two incompatible import resolution strategies. `pipeline/tests/conftest.py:8` adds `pipeline/` to `sys.path` to support `from config.settings import ...` style imports (used by `test_settings.py:19`). But `pipeline/tests/test_database.py:21` uses `from pipeline.src.database import ...` and `pipeline/tests/test_models.py:19` uses `from pipeline.src.models import ...` — both require the project root (`jseeker/`) in `sys.path`, not `pipeline/`. This works when pytest is invoked as `python -m pytest` from the project root (which adds `jseeker/` automatically), but fails with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'pipeline'` if pytest is run from a non-standard CWD, from within a CI container with different defaults, or with `--import-mode=importlib`. `test_settings.py` passes in both cases, masking the issue.

## Affected Surfaces

- `pipeline/tests/conftest.py:8` — adds `pipeline/` to sys.path (from drift review)
- `pipeline/tests/test_database.py:21` — `from pipeline.src.database import ...` requires project root (from drift review)
- `pipeline/tests/test_models.py:19` — `from pipeline.src.models import ...` requires project root (from drift review)
- `pipeline/tests/test_settings.py:19` — `from config.settings import ...` uses conftest approach (reference)

## Fix

Add a `pyproject.toml` at the project root with `[tool.pytest.ini_options] pythonpath = ["."]`. This makes the project root reliably available in sys.path regardless of CWD or import mode, unifying both import strategies without changing any test file.

## Changes Needed

- Add `pyproject.toml` at project root:
  ```toml
  [tool.pytest.ini_options]
  pythonpath = ["."]
  ```
- Optionally remove the manual `sys.path.insert` from `conftest.py:8` if `pythonpath = [".", "pipeline"]` covers both needs.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `pytest pipeline/tests/` run from the repo root passes all tests
- [ ] `cd pipeline && pytest tests/` also passes all tests (or fails with a clear error about the working directory, not a ModuleNotFoundError)
- [ ] `test_database.py`, `test_models.py`, and `test_settings.py` all import their modules without error in both invocation styles

title: Fix normalizer.py: update all Job() calls from raw= to raw_json=json.dumps() after seek-28 rename
description:
## Root Cause

seek-28 renamed the `raw` dataclass field to `raw_json` and added a `@property raw` for backward-compatible reading. However, `pipeline/src/normalizer.py` was not updated alongside `models.py`. The `@dataclass`-generated `__init__` only accepts actual field names — `@property` decorators are not `__init__` parameters. All three normalize functions still pass `raw=<dict>` as a keyword argument to `Job()`:

- `normalize_mock` at line 21: `return Job(..., raw=raw)`
- `normalize_adzuna` at line 52: `return Job(..., raw=raw)`
- `normalize_remoteok` at line 87: `return Job(..., raw=raw)`

This raises `TypeError: Job.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'raw'` on every call. All three normalize functions are completely broken — they fail immediately on invocation. This is the core data transformation path for all currently implemented pipeline sources (mock, Adzuna, RemoteOK). No job data can be ingested until this is fixed.

Additionally, the value `raw=<dict>` would need to become `raw_json=json.dumps(<dict>)` because the field type is `Optional[str]`, not `Optional[dict]`.

## Affected Surfaces

- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:21` — `Job(..., raw=raw)` in `normalize_mock()` (from correctness R2 review)
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:52` — `Job(..., raw=raw)` in `normalize_adzuna()` (from correctness R2 review)
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:87` — `Job(..., raw=raw)` in `normalize_remoteok()` (from correctness R2 review)

## Fix

In all three Job() constructor calls in normalizer.py, change `raw=raw` to `raw_json=json.dumps(raw)`.

## Changes Needed

- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:21`: `raw=raw` → `raw_json=json.dumps(raw)`
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:52`: `raw=raw` → `raw_json=json.dumps(raw)`
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:87`: `raw=raw` → `raw_json=json.dumps(raw)`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `normalize_mock(...)`, `normalize_adzuna(...)`, and `normalize_remoteok(...)` all return a `Job` instance without raising `TypeError`
- [ ] The returned `Job.raw_json` is a JSON string, not a dict
- [ ] `job.raw` property on the returned `Job` returns the original dict
- [ ] All pipeline tests that call a normalize function pass

title: Guard job.raw property against JSONDecodeError on malformed raw_json
description:
## Root Cause

The `raw` backward-compat property introduced in seek-28 at `pipeline/src/models.py:79` calls `json.loads(self.raw_json)` with no exception handling:

```python
return json.loads(self.raw_json) if self.raw_json else None
```

`raw_json` is typed `Optional[str]` with no format enforcement — any string value can be stored. If the field holds invalid JSON (e.g., a truncated API response stored by an error-recovery path, a value written directly to the DB by an external tool, or partial data from a failed serialization), `json.JSONDecodeError` is raised with no application-level context. Any caller reading `job.raw` on such a row receives an unhandled exception rather than `None` or a sentinel. This will manifest once the pipeline begins writing to the DB and reading rows back.

## Affected Surfaces

- `pipeline/src/models.py:79` — `return json.loads(self.raw_json) if self.raw_json else None` (from edge-cases R2 review)

## Fix

Wrap the `json.loads()` call in a try/except:

```python
@property
def raw(self) -> Optional[dict]:
    """Backward-compatible dict view of raw_json; returns None when raw_json is None or invalid."""
    if not self.raw_json:
        return None
    try:
        return json.loads(self.raw_json)
    except json.JSONDecodeError:
        return None
```

## Changes Needed

- `pipeline/src/models.py:76-79`: Replace property body with try/except version above

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `job.raw` returns `None` when `raw_json` is `None` or empty string
- [ ] `job.raw` returns `None` (does not raise) when `raw_json` is malformed JSON
- [ ] `job.raw` returns the correct dict when `raw_json` is valid JSON

title: Future Work
description: Low-priority polish and improvements from review sessions

title: Fix DB_PATH empty-string edge case: use || instead of ?? in web/lib/db.ts
description:
## Root Cause

`web/lib/db.ts:15` uses nullish coalescing (`??`) to fall back to the default DB path:

```typescript
const DB_PATH = process.env.DB_PATH ?? path.resolve(__dirname, "../../data/jobs.db");
```

`??` only short-circuits on `null` or `undefined` — not on empty string. If `DB_PATH` is explicitly set to `""` in the environment (e.g., `DB_PATH= npm run dev` in a shell), `new Database("")` is called. SQLite treats an empty string path as a temporary database — no error is thrown at connection time — but all queries fail silently with "no such table" because the schema is absent. The failure mode is not obvious at the connection site.

## Affected Surfaces

- `web/lib/db.ts:15` — `process.env.DB_PATH ?? path.resolve(...)` (from edge-cases R2 review)

## Fix

Replace `??` with `||` (falsy check, treats empty string as missing):

```typescript
const DB_PATH = process.env.DB_PATH || path.resolve(__dirname, "../../data/jobs.db");
```

## Changes Needed

- `web/lib/db.ts:15`: `??` → `||`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `DB_PATH=""` in environment falls back to the `__dirname`-anchored default path
- [ ] `DB_PATH="/custom/path"` still uses the custom path
- [ ] Unset `DB_PATH` still uses the default path

title: Fix normalizer.py: add missing source_type argument to all three Job() constructor calls
description:
## Root Cause

`source_type: str` was added as a required field on the `Job` dataclass in the seek-2 V2 model changes (`351ce4c`). `pipeline/src/normalizer.py` is a V1 port that was never updated for V2 required fields. All three normalize functions construct `Job(...)` without passing `source_type`:

- `normalize_mock` (line 16): `Job(..., raw_json=json.dumps(raw))` — no `source_type`
- `normalize_adzuna` (line 44): `Job(..., raw_json=json.dumps(raw))` — no `source_type`
- `normalize_remoteok` (line 79): `Job(..., raw_json=json.dumps(raw))` — no `source_type`

Every invocation of any normalize function raises:
```
TypeError: Job.__init__() missing 1 required positional argument: 'source_type'
```

The pipeline cannot normalize any job data until this is fixed. This is a pre-existing V1→V2 migration gap, not introduced by any recent fix commit. seek-4 does not cover normalizer.py (its scope is fetcher files only).

Found independently by both the Correctness and Edge Cases reviewers in Round 3.

## Affected Surfaces

- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:16` — `normalize_mock()` Job() call missing `source_type` (from correctness R3 + edge-cases R3)
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:44` — `normalize_adzuna()` Job() call missing `source_type` (from correctness R3 + edge-cases R3)
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:79` — `normalize_remoteok()` Job() call missing `source_type` (from correctness R3 + edge-cases R3)

## Fix

Add `source_type=` to each Job() constructor call:

```python
# normalize_mock (line ~16):
return Job(..., raw_json=json.dumps(raw), source_type="mock")

# normalize_adzuna (line ~44):
return Job(..., raw_json=json.dumps(raw), source_type="api")
```
